standardRefs: NYS Chemistry

questions.prompt: Chemistry - Atomic Structure: which concept is the best anchor for a Regents-style item?
questions.correctText: proton
choices: proton
neutron
atomic radius
group trend
questions.explanation: proton is the anchor concept for Atomic Structure. In NYS Chemistry, students need to use that concept to reason through a task, not just recognize the word.

standardRefs: NYS Chemistry
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student reviewing Chemistry: Atomic Structure needs to show the skill "compare atoms." Which action best fits that skill?
questions.correctText: Use evidence to explain neutron.
choices: Use evidence to explain neutron.
Guess from key words about electron.
ignoring the source or data in the prompt
Repeat the definition of proton only.
questions.explanation: The useful move is to connect evidence to neutron; this matches the standards/CED emphasis on reasoning and application.

standardRefs: NYS Chemistry
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which answer best avoids a common mistake about Chemistry: Atomic Structure?

choices: Choose an unrelated fact from another unit.
using a rule from a different unit
Connect proton to the prompt context before choosing.
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
questions.explanation: Most missed review questions come from using familiar terms without matching them to the exact context.

standardRefs: NYS Chemistry

A fact from a different course.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
questions.explanation: The standards across NYS courses and AP CEDs reward evidence used for a claim, not disconnected recall.
